title: Word Ladder II
problem_statement: Return all shortest transformation sequences from beginWord to endWord using a dictionary of words.

examples.explanation: There are two shortest transformation sequences.

constraints: All words are the same length and lowercase.
intuition: Find shortest path length with BFS, then backtrack through a parent graph to build all minimum sequences.

approaches.name: BFS + backtracking
approaches.description: Build neighbor relationships at shortest distances with BFS, then DFS from endWord to beginWord through valid predecessors.
approaches.why_not: A naive DFS can generate non-shortest sequences and is too slow for all shortest paths.

interview_qa.a: Use BFS to determine shortest distances, then backtrack to build all paths constrained to those distances.
BFS finds distance but does not enumerate all shortest sequences without parent tracking.
